description: The Young Lords were a far-left political organization that evolved from a Chicago street gang founded in 1959. Known for direct action campaigns and community service programs, they advocated for Puerto Rican independence and socialism. Major branches existed in Chicago and New York City, with the New York chapter later becoming the Young Lords Party. The organization dissolved in 1972 due to internal splits and FBI counterintelligence operations.

country_links.evidence_quote: The YLP expanded into Puerto Rico in 1971... by the end of March, the YLP had devoted significant resources to relocating, with 20 members being transferred to the Puerto Rican branch.
country_links.context: The Young Lords Party expanded into Puerto Rico, transferring members and establishing a branch there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Connecticut, the Young Lords established chapters in both Bridgeport and New Haven... A Young Lords chapter was founded in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in 1970... Young Lords chapters were also founded in Boston, Massachusetts, as well as Hoboken, Jersey City, and Newark, New Jersey.
country_links.context: The Young Lords operated chapters and carried out actions in multiple U.S. cities including New York, Chicago, Philadelphia, Boston, and others.
